title: Tip Calculator
description: Industrial-grade tip calculator for service transactions, incorporating lean efficiency and Six Sigma accuracy standards.

inputs.label: Bill Amount
inputs.businessContext: Total pre-tax bill amount for the service transaction.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total pre-tax bill amount for the service transaction.

inputs.label: Service Quality Rating
inputs.businessContext: Customer's perception of service quality, used to determine base tip percentage.

businessContext_i18n.en: Customer's perception of service quality, used to determine base tip percentage.

inputs.label: Party Size
inputs.businessContext: Number of people in the dining party, affects automatic gratuity thresholds.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of people in the dining party, affects automatic gratuity thresholds.

inputs.label: Include Tax in Tip Calculation
inputs.businessContext: If true, tip is calculated on bill amount including tax; otherwise on pre-tax amount.

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, tip is calculated on bill amount including tax; otherwise on pre-tax amount.

inputs.label: Tax Amount
inputs.businessContext: Applicable sales tax or VAT on the bill, used when include_tax_in_tip is true.

businessContext_i18n.en: Applicable sales tax or VAT on the bill, used when include_tax_in_tip is true.

inputs.label: Round Up Tip to Nearest Dollar
inputs.businessContext: If true, the final tip amount is rounded up to the next whole dollar.

label_i18n.en: Round Up Tip to Nearest Dollar

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, the final tip amount is rounded up to the next whole dollar.

rules.message: Bill amount must be greater than zero.
Party size must be at least 1.
Tax amount must be non-negative when including tax.
Service quality must be one of: poor, average, good, excellent.

thresholds.message: Tip amount exceeds $100, consider reviewing service quality.
Party size of 8 or more may be subject to automatic gratuity per restaurant policy.
Tip percentage is below 10%, which may be considered low for standard service.

formulas.description: Determine base tip percentage from service quality rating (ISO 9001 customer satisfaction mapping).
formulas.expression: CASE service_quality WHEN 'poor' THEN 10 WHEN 'average' THEN 15 WHEN 'good' THEN 18 WHEN 'excellent' THEN 20 ELSE 15 END

formulas.description: Adjust tip percentage for party size using lean efficiency factor (larger parties may warrant higher tip due to complexity).
formulas.expression: base_tip_percentage + (CASE WHEN party_size >= 6 THEN 2 ELSE 0 END)

formulas.description: Calculate the bill amount on which tip is based, including tax if selected.
formulas.expression: CASE WHEN include_tax_in_tip == true THEN bill_amount + tax_amount ELSE bill_amount END

formulas.description: Compute raw tip amount before rounding.
Apply rounding if selected, producing the final tip amount.
formulas.expression: CASE WHEN round_up == true THEN CEIL(raw_tip_amount) ELSE ROUND(raw_tip_amount, 2) END

formulas.description: Total amount paid including bill, tax, and tip.
Actual tip percentage relative to original bill amount (excluding tax) for reporting.

primary.label: Recommended Tip Amount
primary.description: Final tip amount calculated based on inputs and rounding preference.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Rounding Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Difference between raw tip and final tip due to rounding, representing potential loss/gain.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Tax Inclusion Effect
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Additional tip amount generated by including tax in the base, representing hidden cost to customer.
hiddenLossDrivers.formula: CASE WHEN include_tax_in_tip == true THEN (tax_amount * adjusted_tip_percentage / 100) ELSE 0 END

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Party Size Premium
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Extra tip percentage added due to party size adjustment, representing incremental service cost.
hiddenLossDrivers.formula: CASE WHEN party_size >= 6 THEN 2 ELSE 0 END

suggestedActions.action: Consider discussing service concerns with management to improve future experiences.
Consider splitting the tip equally among party members for convenience.
Verify if automatic gratuity has already been added to the bill before adding additional tip.
Consider rounding up the tip to the nearest dollar for simplicity.
